work_unit: H-002 derived fix: pipeline.py shell injection via state['min_bytes'] f-string interpolation
situation: audit-2026-07-06-022 finding H-002 (high severity). pipeline.py:222 cmd = f"WECHAT_MIN_BYTES={state['min_bytes']} {cmd}" interpolates state JSON value directly into shell command prefix. State file (_load at line 64) trusts arbitrary JSON without schema validation (M-010). Agent-loop executes STEP.cmd via bash (SKILL.md line 40), so attacker-controllable state value can trigger command substitution ($(...) or backticks). _run_gate (line 87-110) already correctly uses env dict — line 222 is a redundant dangerous parallel path.
action: Route-fix: removed f-string shell prefix from pipeline.py cmd_next; added STEP.env dict output with int-cast + default fallback for malformed state. Updated SKILL.md agent-loop contract to pass WECHAT_MIN_BYTES via env dict instead of relying on cmd prefix. Both changes committed in single worktree branch.
outcome_evidence: Commit abc517d on auto/opt-wechat-main-1783130541 (2 files changed, 12 insertions, 4 deletions). Pushed to origin. pipeline.py no longer interpolates state['min_bytes'] into shell cmd; outputs STEP.env.WECHAT_MIN_BYTES as sanitized int string. SKILL.md agent-loop updated to use env dict.
next_suggestion: Dispatch H-003 (bing_search SSRF) as next high-severity derived fix from audit-2026-07-06-022
After H-003..H-005 dispatched, batch medium-severity M-001..M-017 by file grouping (qq_push.py has 4 findings — combine into single worktree)

audit_findings.finding: Shell injection via f-string interpolation of state['min_bytes'] into bash cmd prefix. State JSON unvalidated (M-010); agent-loop executes STEP.cmd via bash → command substitution possible if state poisoned.
audit_findings.remediation: Pass WECHAT_MIN_BYTES via subprocess env dict (as _run_gate already does). Int-cast + default fallback for malformed state. Output STEP.env for agent-loop consumption.
